clusters.title: Details on U.S. attack and seizure of Iranian cargo ship
clusters.neutral_headline: What we know about the US capture of Iranian vessel near Hormuz
bullet_summary: The #US said it had seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get past its naval blockade near the Strait of #Hormuz on Sunday
President Trump said a U
The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said

items.title: Details on U.S. attack and seizure of Iranian cargo ship
items.standfirst: U.S. forces fired on and then seized an Iranian vessel over the weekend, intensifying the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie D'Agata has more.
items.content: U.S. forces fired on and then seized an Iranian vessel over the weekend, intensifying the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie D'Agata has more.

items.title: US seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
items.standfirst: The #US said it had seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get past its naval blockade near the Strait of #Hormuz on Sunday. #Iran vowed to retaliate, throwing planned peace talks between the two countries into question.
items.content: The #US said it had seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get past its naval blockade near the Strait of #Hormuz on Sunday. #Iran vowed to retaliate, throwing planned peace talks between the two countries into question.

items.title: What we know about the US capture of Iranian vessel near Hormuz
items.standfirst: US releases video showing the capture of Iranian-flagged ship near Hormuz, Trump says it tried to breach its blockade.
items.content: US releases video showing the capture of Iranian-flagged ship near Hormuz, Trump says it tried to breach its blockade.

items.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
items.standfirst: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
items.standfirst: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.
items.content: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)

items.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
items.standfirst: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.
items.content: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

clusters.title: Humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record in Beijing
clusters.neutral_headline: Humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record in Beijing
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, Bloomberg Technology and others

items.title: Humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record in Beijing
items.standfirst: Some robots ran the Beijing half-marathon autonomously, while others were controlled remotely, all competing on a parallel course to avoid collisions with human athletes.
items.content: Some robots ran the Beijing half-marathon autonomously, while others were controlled remotely, all competing on a parallel course to avoid collisions with human athletes.

items.title: Robot beats human half-marathon world record
items.standfirst: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.
items.content: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

items.title: China Robot’s 50-Minute Half Marathon Shows Pace of AI Progress
items.standfirst: On Sunday morning in a Beijing suburb, a red, humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — about seven minutes faster than the men’s world record.
items.content: On Sunday morning in a Beijing suburb, a red, humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — about seven minutes faster than the men’s world record.

items.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
items.standfirst: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...
items.content: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...

items.title: Humanoid robot runs a half-marathon race faster than the human world record
items.standfirst: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.
items.content: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.

clusters.title: Tariff refund portal off to bumpy start as some businesses report glitches
clusters.neutral_headline: US launches tariff refund system as thousands of importers line up
bullet_summary: importers reported problems filing tariff refund claims after Customs and Border Patrol launched its dedicated portal on Monday
The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments
The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow

items.title: Tariff refund portal off to bumpy start as some businesses report glitches
items.standfirst: Some U.S. importers reported problems filing tariff refund claims after Customs and Border Patrol launched its dedicated portal on Monday.
items.content: Some U.S. importers reported problems filing tariff refund claims after Customs and Border Patrol launched its dedicated portal on Monday.

items.title: US launches tariff refund system as thousands of importers line up
items.standfirst: More than 330,000 importers paid up to $166bn in tariffs on 53 million shipments ​of imported goods.
items.content: More than 330,000 importers paid up to $166bn in tariffs on 53 million shipments ​of imported goods.

items.title: US tariff refund system launches for companies to claim billions in import taxes
items.standfirst: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.
items.content: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.

items.title: Trump administration begins refunding more than $166bn in tariffs
items.standfirst: Claims system launches months after supreme court ruled Trump had no legal authority to impose tariffsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe Trump administration has begun accepting applications from businesses seeking refunds for more than $166bn in tariffs, months after the supreme court ruled that the president had no legal authority to impose them.The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow. Continue reading...
items.content: Claims system launches months after supreme court ruled Trump had no legal authority to impose tariffsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe Trump administration has begun accepting applications from businesses seeking refunds for more than $166bn in tariffs, months after the supreme court ruled that the president had no legal authority to impose them.The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Musk Snubs French in Case Over Grok Sexual Images, AFP Says
clusters.neutral_headline: Elon Musk snubs Paris legal summons over alleged child abuse images on X
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Guardian World, France24

items.title: Musk Snubs French in Case Over Grok Sexual Images, AFP Says
items.standfirst: Elon Musk snubbed a summons by French prosecutors investigating how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content, according to Agence France-Presse.
items.content: Elon Musk snubbed a summons by French prosecutors investigating how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content, according to Agence France-Presse.

items.title: Elon Musk snubs Paris legal summons over alleged child abuse images on X
items.standfirst: Billionaire owner elects not to attend voluntary interview as part of investigation by French cybercrime unitElon Musk did not appear on Monday for a voluntary interview with lawyers in Paris, who had summoned the American tech billionaire over an investigation into his social media platform X and AI chatbot Grok.The prosecutors told AFP that they had “taken note of the absence of the first people summoned”, without mentioning Musk’s name. The billionaire called the French authorities involved “retards” weeks earlier in a French-language post on X. Continue reading...
items.content: Billionaire owner elects not to attend voluntary interview as part of investigation by French cybercrime unitElon Musk did not appear on Monday for a voluntary interview with lawyers in Paris, who had summoned the American tech billionaire over an investigation into his social media platform X and AI chatbot Grok.The prosecutors told AFP that they had “taken note of the absence of the first people summoned”, without mentioning Musk’s name. The billionaire called the French authorities involved “retards” weeks earlier in a French-language post on X. Continue reading...

items.title: Why is French cybercrime unit investigating Elon Musk's X?
items.standfirst: Elon Musk's summons ​for questioning by prosecutors in Paris is linked to a widened investigation into alleged offences linked to X's artificial intelligence chatbot Grok, abuse of ​algorithms and fraudulent data extraction. The probe is being led by the cybercrime division of the Paris prosecutor’s office. FRANCE 24's Delano D'Souza tells us more.
items.content: Elon Musk's summons ​for questioning by prosecutors in Paris is linked to a widened investigation into alleged offences linked to X's artificial intelligence chatbot Grok, abuse of ​algorithms and fraudulent data extraction. The probe is being led by the cybercrime division of the Paris prosecutor’s office. FRANCE 24's Delano D'Souza tells us more.

clusters.neutral_headline: AST Drops as Blue Origin Rocket Places Satellite in Wrong Orbit
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Reuters Top News, CBS News

items.title: AST Drops as Blue Origin Rocket Places Satellite in Wrong Orbit
items.standfirst: AST SpaceMobile Inc. shares plunged in early trading on Monday after Blue Origin’s flagship New Glenn rocket failed to correctly place a payload it was carrying for the Texas-based satellite networking company into its intended orbit.
items.content: AST SpaceMobile Inc. shares plunged in early trading on Monday after Blue Origin’s flagship New Glenn rocket failed to correctly place a payload it was carrying for the Texas-based satellite networking company into its intended orbit.

items.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn reuses booster but upper stage misses orbit
items.standfirst: Blue Origin achieved its first New Glenn booster reuse, but the rocket’s upper stage put the payload into the wrong orbit, undercutting the launch’s overall success. [arstechnica.com]
items.content: Blue Origin achieved its first New Glenn booster reuse, but the rocket’s upper stage put the payload into the wrong orbit, undercutting the launch’s overall success. [arstechnica.com]

items.title: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket puts satellite payload into wrong orbit
items.standfirst: The launching appeared to go off without a hitch, but a problem prevented the rocket's upper stage from putting its payload into the correct orbit.
items.content: The launching appeared to go off without a hitch, but a problem prevented the rocket's upper stage from putting its payload into the correct orbit.

clusters.title: Kremlin-friendly ex-president Rumen Radev scores big election win in Bulgaria
clusters.neutral_headline: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Kremlin-friendly ex-president Rumen Radev scores big election win in Bulgaria
items.standfirst: The center-left coalition of ex-president Rumen Radev has emerged as the clear winner of Bulgaria's parliamentary election, the country's central electoral commission said Monday, ending half a decade of political fragmentation.
items.content: The center-left coalition of ex-president Rumen Radev has emerged as the clear winner of Bulgaria's parliamentary election, the country's central electoral commission said Monday, ending half a decade of political fragmentation.

items.title: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
items.standfirst: Former president’s win could end years of weak coalition governments but critics say it may be bad for EUBulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues.With all votes counted on Monday, the Progressive Bulgaria party of Rumen Radev, a former fighter pilot and air force chief, had 44.6% of the vote, giving it an estimated 131 of the 240 seats in the national assembly. Continue reading...
items.content: Former president’s win could end years of weak coalition governments but critics say it may be bad for EUBulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues.With all votes counted on Monday, the Progressive Bulgaria party of Rumen Radev, a former fighter pilot and air force chief, had 44.6% of the vote, giving it an estimated 131 of the 240 seats in the national assembly. clusters.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old girl found in car
clusters.neutral_headline: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old girl found in car
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old girl found in car
items.standfirst: Musician has been charged after the dismembered and decomposing body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez was found in abandoned TeslaThe singer D4vd has been charged with the murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the teenage girl whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in the artist’s apparently abandoned Tesla in September.The Los Angeles county district attorney’s office said the 21-year-old, whose legal name is David Burke, was charged with first-degree murder in the killing of Rivas Hernandez, who was reported missing by her family in 2024, when she was 13. Authorities say she was 14 when she died. Continue reading...
items.content: Musician has been charged after the dismembered and decomposing body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez was found in abandoned TeslaThe singer D4vd has been charged with the murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the teenage girl whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in the artist’s apparently abandoned Tesla in September.The Los Angeles county district attorney’s office said the 21-year-old, whose legal name is David Burke, was charged with first-degree murder in the killing of Rivas Hernandez, who was reported missing by her family in 2024, when she was 13. Authorities say she was 14 when she died. Continue reading...

items.title: D4vd charged with murder in connection with death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
items.standfirst: D4vd, whose real name is David Burke, is charged with first-degree murder with special circumstances, lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14 and mutilating a body.
items.content: D4vd, whose real name is David Burke, is charged with first-degree murder with special circumstances, lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14 and mutilating a body.

items.title: Singer d4vd charged with first-degree murder in 14-year-old girl's death, DA announces
items.standfirst: Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman announced singer d4vd has been charged with first-degree murder in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose dismembered body was found in a Tesla registered to the musician last September.
items.content: Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman announced singer d4vd has been charged with first-degree murder in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose dismembered body was found in a Tesla registered to the musician last September.

items.title: Woman charged with attempted murder after car hit pedestrians in London
items.standfirst: Gabrielle Carrington, 29, faces charges after incident on Argyll Street in the early hours of Sunday morningA woman has been charged with attempted murder after a car hit pedestrians in central London in the early hours of Sunday.A woman in her 30s remains in a life-threatening condition and a man in his 50s suffered life-changing injuries after they were hit by a car in Argyll Street, Westminster, at approximately 4.30am on Sunday, the Metropolitan police said. A second woman in her 30s suffered minor injuries, the force added. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
clusters.neutral_headline: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NBC World

items.title: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
items.standfirst: The Iran regime said Monday that it has no plans to attend peace talks in Pakistan with President Trump's top three negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, as Tehran balks at what it considers "unreasonable and unrealistic demands" by the White House. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand has more.
items.content: The Iran regime said Monday that it has no plans to attend peace talks in Pakistan with President Trump's top three negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, as Tehran balks at what it considers "unreasonable and unrealistic demands" by the White House. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand has more.

items.title: President Trump says U.S. struck & seized Iranian tanker
items.standfirst: President Trump announcing the U.S. Navy struck and seized an Iranian tanker attempting to cross the U.S.’ blockade. It comes as the U.S.’ ceasefire with Iran is in jeopardy. The White House says negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, will return to Pakistan tomorrow for a new round of peace talks with Iran. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports from Tel Aviv.
items.content: President Trump announcing the U.S. Navy struck and seized an Iranian tanker attempting to cross the U.S.’ blockade. It comes as the U.S.’ ceasefire with Iran is in jeopardy. The White House says negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, will return to Pakistan tomorrow for a new round of peace talks with Iran. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports from Tel Aviv.
